# DOCA AI Guidance

Applies to: DOCA source packages and package-local agent entrypoints
Read when: an agent needs a compact source-package starting point before loading `top-level guidance directories`
Load next: `getting-started/quickstart.md`, `getting-started/README.md`, `skills/doca-user-rules/SKILL.md`, `skills/doca-ai-runner/SKILL.md`

This is the compact LLM entrypoint for DOCA source packages. It points agents to the source-backed guidance, contracts,
skills, and read-only tools that are safe to use before proposing builds, runtime commands, or source edits.

## Start Here

- `AGENTS.md`: root entrypoint for agent routing.
- `reference/README.md`: repository-wide source, build, and validation rules.
- `getting-started/quickstart.md`: package-facing onboarding.
- `skills/doca-user-rules/SKILL.md`: base behavior for DOCA source-package agents.
- `skills/doca-ai-runner/SKILL.md`: read-only discovery, capability lookup, and package planning commands.
- `skills/doca-discover-environment/SKILL.md`: capability and environment discovery without hardcoded topology
  assumptions.
- `skills/doca-programming-guide/SKILL.md`: DOCA Programming Guide routing enriched with local source evidence.
- `guides/capability-map.md`: samples and applications capability map.
- `skills/doca-explorer/SKILL.md`: samples and applications exploration workflow and response shape.

## Machine-Readable Entry Points

- `contracts/agent-manifest.json`: task and capability index.
- `contracts/capability-catalog.json`: compact capability summaries.
- `contracts/tasks/discover-doca-environment.yaml`: read-only environment discovery task.
- `contracts/tasks/build-sdk-sample.yaml`: package-local build planning task.

## First Commands

Run these from the source-package root before making environment or API claims:

```bash
python3 tools/lookup_capability.py --repo-root . --list
python3 tools/run_agent_task.py --task discover-doca-environment --repo-root .
```

For API or lifecycle answers, list package-visible capabilities first, then use source-backed capability lookup:

```bash
python3 tools/lookup_capability.py --repo-root . --api-index <capability-id>
```

For build requests, use planner-only mode first:

```bash
python3 tools/run_agent_task.py --task build-sdk-sample --repo-root . --focus-path samples/doca_flow
```

## Safety Boundaries

Do not install system packages, mutate device state, change network or credential configuration, write persistent user
configuration, run live traffic, or execute runtime samples unless the local owner explicitly approves that action
class. Report missing prerequisites and the exact next safe command instead of hiding environment gaps.
